 Today, we're introducing Claude Opus 4.8 
<https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-8?utm_content=link>, our most 
intelligent Opus model and the best generally available model for coding, 
agents, and demanding knowledge work.


 Here's what's new on the Claude Platform (API): 


 * Claude Opus 4.8: The latest version of Opus, with improvements in coding, 
long-running autonomous agents, and reasoning on complex enterprise problems. 
Available at $5 input / $25 output per million tokens. 
 * Fast mode (research preview): Fast mode for Opus 4.8 delivers full Opus 4.8 
intelligence with roughly 2.5x faster output token generation. Fast mode now 
costs less than before, at 2x standard Opus 4.8 pricing. Reach out to your 
account team to get started or request accesshere 
<https://claude.com/fast-mode?utm_content=inline_link>. 
 * Mid-conversation system messages 
<https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/mid-conversation-system-messages?utm_content=inline_link>
: The Messages API now accepts system entries inside the messages array, so you 
can update Claude's instructions mid-task without breaking the prompt cache. 
 * Self-hosted sandboxes for Claude Managed Agents (public beta) 
<https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed-agents/self-hosted-sandboxes?utm_content=inline_link>
: Build and deploy agents at scale while keeping sensitive files, packages, and 
services in your own infrastructure or with a managed sandbox provider. 
 * MCP tunnels (research preview) 
<https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/mcp-tunnels/overview?utm_content=inline_link>
: Connect agents to MCP servers inside your private network without exposing 
them to the public internet. Request accesshere 
<https://claude.com/form/claude-managed-agents?utm_content=inline_link>. 


 For Claude Code users: 


 *  Claude Opus 4.8 is now the default fallback model 
<https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config?utm_content=inline_link> in 
Claude Code. It defaults to high effort, which is the best balance of quality 
and speed, costing roughly the same as Opus 4.7 on xhigh effort. 
 * Dynamic workflows 
<https://claude.com/blog/introducing-dynamic-workflows-in-claude-code?utm_content=inline_link>
 are now available in Claude Code, which help Claude tackle extraordinarily 
large tasks, executing across 10s to 100s of parallel subagents. They are on by 
default, and can be toggled off by adding “disableWorkflows: true” to your 
managed settings. See the blog for more details.

subject: 30 seconds to your first response
body_plain:
Two code blocks. Pick Python or curl. Done in 30 seconds.  

OpenRouter
OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai/?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=B1-1+First+Response&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

Hi Lance,

The fastest path from signup to a working response is two code blocks. Copy one, swap your key, run it. If you already use the OpenAI SDK, the only line that changes is base_url.

Open the quickstart ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/quickstart?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=b1_first_request&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=B1-1+First+Response&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

PYTHON

from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI
(   base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
  api_key="YOUR_API_KEY" )


response = client.chat.completions.create
(   model="openai/gpt-4o-mini",
  messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}] )

print(response.choices[0].message.content)

 

CURL

curl https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model":"openai/gpt-4o-mini","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Hello"}]}'

 

What the response looks like. A standard OpenAI chat completion object. choices[0].message.content holds the text. usage has token counts. model shows which model actually served the request, which matters when you use openrouter/auto and want to see what the router picked. If you already read OpenAI responses, you already know how to read these.

If the first call fails. A 401 is almost always the key header. A 402 means the free-tier daily cap was hit or credits are needed for a paid model. A 404 on a model string usually means dots vs hyphens; OpenRouter slugs use dots, so claude-sonnet-4.6 not claude-sonnet-4-6. The models ( https://openrouter.ai/models?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=b1_first_request&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=B1-1+First+Response&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ) page has every valid slug.

Free tier: 10 requests per day across 29 free models. No credit card to start.

Getting Started

1. Create your API key ( https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys?utm_source=customerio&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=checklist&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=B1-1+First+Response&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

2. Point the OpenAI SDK at openrouter.ai/api/v1

3. Make your first request ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/quickstart?utm_source=customerio&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=checklist&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=B1-1+First+Response&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

PICK MODELS LATER

Once a single request works, you can let the router choose models for you. openrouter/auto picks per-request based on the prompt. The only change is the model string. Roughly 63 percent of auto traffic routes to models under $1 per million tokens, so simple tasks stop hitting expensive models without any routing code on your side.

response = client.chat.completions.create
(   model="openrouter/auto",
  messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}], )

subject: Welcome to OpenRouter
body_plain:
One API key, 300+ models. Create yours and start making requests.  

OpenRouter
OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai/?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A1+Welcome&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

Hi Lance,

Welcome to OpenRouter. You now have one API key that reaches 300+ models across OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, ArjunSeek, xAI, Mistral, and every other major provider. It is drop-in compatible with the OpenAI SDK, so the integration looks identical to what you already have, and you stop maintaining a separate account and a separate SDK for each provider.

Create your API key ( https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=welcome&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A1+Welcome&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

One key, one billing surface, one rate limit pool, one place to see usage. When a provider has a bad hour, OpenRouter falls over to the next provider serving the same model, so your app does not cascade failures when OpenAI or Anthropic goes down. When a new model drops, it is a string change, not an integration project.

Getting Started

1. Create your API key ( https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys?utm_source=customerio&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=checklist&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A1+Welcome&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

2. Point the OpenAI SDK at openrouter.ai/api/v1

3. Make your first request ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/quickstart?utm_source=customerio&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=checklist&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A1+Welcome&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

SWITCHING MODELS

Switch from openai/gpt-4o-mini to anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6 to openrouter/auto by changing one string in your API call. No new SDK, no new credentials, no second billing account. Browse all 300+ models ( https://openrouter.ai/models?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=a1_models_link&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A1+Welcome&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ).

openrouter/auto is worth knowing from day one. It reads the prompt and picks a model per request, so you can ship without committing to a specific provider. Roughly 63 percent of auto traffic routes to models under $1 per million tokens, which is how simple prompts stop hitting expensive models. See how auto-routing works ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/routing/routers/auto-router?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=a1_auto_routing_link&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A1+Welcome&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ).

subject: Re: Introduction: Advantage + Eragon
body_plain:
Hi Lance,

Nice to meet you, and appreciate Kenny looping you in here as the new POC
for the SOC 2 initiative.

Kenny and I connected last week to walk through Eragon’s current setup and
what the audit process would look like with Advantage Partners. From our
conversation, it sounds like the team has already completed the readiness
work in Vanta and is in a good position to move forward fairly quickly.

Quick recap of what we discussed:

- SOC 2 Type 2 audit pricing: $5,000
- Estimated timeline: ~4–4.5 months total
- 3-month observation period
- ~4–6 weeks for testing, reporting, and issuance afterward

We also covered how we approach the process:
- Readiness review and onboarding assistance included
- Pre-audit review before the observation period begins
- Hands-on support throughout the audit so there are no surprises during
the observation window
- Tight alignment with Vanta and their workflows, which helps streamline
the process considerably for lean teams like yours

We additionally discussed optional penetration testing services, and I
shared black box / gray box examples along with our scoping form:
https://ap-pentest-scoping.zapier.app/page

As another reference point, here’s an example Vanta trust center showing
what the end result can look like after the audit is complete:
https://app.vanta.com/dust.tt/trust/f3ytzxpay31bwsiyuqjto

At this stage, I mainly wanted to introduce myself and get a sense of which
direction you’re leaning on the audit side and whether there are any
remaining questions I can help answer.

Looking forward to working with you.

Best,
Andrew T

subject: Re: [Ripple x Eragon] Sample Workflows

Analyse zoominfo vendor contract. Start by searching my Gmail (last 90
days) and Slack for any conversations involving this vendor: extract
pricing discussed, commitments made, and internal sentiment. Then extract
all commercial terms from the contract (TCV, payment terms, auto-renewal,
price cap). Check compliance against our 3 non-negotiables: auto-renewal
opt-out, minimum Net 30 payment terms, and renewal price cap ≤3% or CPI.
Flag any items requiring Legal review. Then give me a prioritised
negotiation strategy with 3–5 levers: specific asks, rationale, and
estimated impact, informed by what you found in Gmail and Slack. Close with
a Final Verdict: Approved, Approved with Conditions, or Blocked.

subject: Stop writing repair code for malformed JSON
body_plain:
JSON Schema enforcement at decode time, across 244 of 357 catalog models.  

OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai?utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

*******************************************************************************************************
One response_format schema, enforced at decode time across 244 of 357 models, refused on the other 113.
*******************************************************************************************************

Ask a model for JSON and you often get prose or malformed output, so the schema ends up living in prompt-and-retry loops and the repair code that catches what retry misses. Send a response_format with a JSON Schema and OpenRouter enforces it at decode time across 244 of 357 catalog models.

Switch the model slug and the schema contract holds: form auto-fill and entity extraction, classification and routing decisions, agent state machines and function arguments all parse the same way.

{
"messages": [
{ "role": "user", "content": "What's the weather like in London?" }
], "response_format": { "type": "json_schema", "json_schema": { "name": "weather", "strict": true, "schema": { "type": "object", "properties": { "location": { "type": "string", "description": "City or location name"
}, "temperature": { "type": "number", "description": "Temperature in Celsius"
}, "conditions": { "type": "string", "description": "Weather conditions description"
}
}, "required": ["location", "temperature", "conditions"], "additionalProperties": false
}
}
}
}

Read the structured outputs guide ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/structured-outputs?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_structured&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

-----------------------
Pairs with tool calling
-----------------------

Tool calling ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/tool-calling?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_structured_support_a&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ) uses the same one-parameter pattern as structured output. Pass a tools array, the model returns conforming JSON, no wrapper code. Same model slug, same endpoint.

The headless agent recipe ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/cookbook/building-agents/create-headless-agent?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_structured_support_b&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ) wires both into a production loop that runs without a UI. Structured output for the model's response shape, tool calling for the actions in between.

Read the headless agent recipe ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/cookbook/building-agents/create-headless-agent?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_structured_secondary_cta&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Latest from the blog

Human-in-the-Loop Tools for the Agent SDK ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/human-in-the-loop-tools?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_hitl_blog_1&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Human-in-the-Loop Tools for the Agent SDK ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/human-in-the-loop-tools?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_hitl_blog_1&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )
A new tool type that lets agents auto-resolve routine decisions and pause for human input on critical ones.

Read the announcement → ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/human-in-the-loop-tools?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_hitl_blog_1&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Consistent web search and fetch across every model ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/agentic-web-tools?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_web_blog_2&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Consistent web search and fetch across every model ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/agentic-web-tools?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_web_blog_2&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )
Tool-calling models get web search and page-fetching, normalized across providers.

See the tools → ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/agentic-web-tools?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_web_blog_2&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Response caching: zero cost for identical requests ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/response-caching?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_cache_blog_3&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Response caching: zero cost for identical requests ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/response-caching?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_cache_blog_3&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )
Cache identical requests at zero token cost with one header. Dramatically faster, free.

Read the launch → ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/response-caching?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_cache_blog_3&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

More shipping soon. Full changelog lives at openrouter.ai/announcements. subject: Welcome to Langfuse
body_plain:
Hi Lance Mathias,
 
Welcome to Langfuse, glad that you signed up!
 
I'm Marc, one of the founders and here to help in case you have any questions.
 
Langfuse has three core pillars:
 * Observability – trace every LLM call, chain steps and cost on one timeline.

 * Prompt management – version‑control prompts and roll them out without redeploys.

 * Evaluations – auto‑ or human‑grade results once you have traffic to benchmark.

 
Watch a short intro: Youtube link [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zzOlFH0iD0k&list=PL4v4zZF1rGG6Bmt3dgtQc31Byzy97t0xr]

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zzOlFH0iD0k&list=PL4v4zZF1rGG6Bmt3dgtQc31Byzy97t0xr

 
Or check out the interactive demo [https://langfuse.com/docs/demo] (you have access in your account) and play with the chatbot to
see your traces in the app
 
Start by using AI Agents
 
Just copy these prompts to let your coding agent do the work:
 * Tracing: ‘Please follow the instructions on this page to set up tracing for my project:
   https://langfuse.com/docs/observability/get-started.md
   [https://langfuse.com/docs/observability/get-started.md?ref=onboarding_emails__1_platform_overview]’ leveraging the Langfuse
   skill https://github.com/langfuse/skills [https://github.com/langfuse/skills]'

 * Prompt Management: ‘Please follow the instructions on this page to set up prompt management for my project:
   https://langfuse.com/docs/prompt-management/get-started.md
   [https://langfuse.com/docs/prompt-management/get-started.md?ref=onboarding_emails__1_platform_overview]leveraging the Langfuse
   skill https://github.com/langfuse/skills [https://github.com/langfuse/skills]'

 
Start with manual Setup
 * Quickstart to start tracing your application
   [https://langfuse.com/docs/observability/get-started?ref=onboarding_emails__1_platform_overview]

 * Quickstart to get started with prompt management
   [https://langfuse.com/docs/prompt-management/get-started?ref=onboarding_emails__1_platform_overview]

 
Need help? Just hit reply, I read every message or book a call with an engineer
[https://cal.com/team/langfuse/welcome-to-langfuse] to chat about your use case.
 
Be part of the open-source community:
 * Vote on community submissions and add your own ideas on our Github Discussions
   [https://github.com/orgs/langfuse/discussions/categories/ideas]

 * Check out the roadmap [https://langfuse.com/docs/roadmap]

 * If you like our work, consider leaving a star ⭐ [https://github.com/langfuse/langfuse]

 * Join our weekly Virtual Community Hours [https://luma.com/langfuse?]

 
--
Marc Klingen
Co-founder & CEO, Langfuse

[https://langfuse.com/?utm_source=transactional&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=welcome_to_langfuse_cloud]@marcklingen
[https://x.com/marcklingen]
 
You are receiving this email because you opted in to receive email from the list Onboarding Journey from Langfuse
Langfuse, Langfuse GmbH, Oranienburger Strasse 91, 10178, Berlin, Germany
Unsubscribe [***REDACTED***

subject: openrouter/auto: per-request model routing
body_plain:
One model name. The router picks per request based on task complexity. No routing fee.  

OpenRouter
OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai/?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A3+Auto+Router&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

Hi Lance,

Picking the right model for every request is a job. openrouter/auto is the model name you use when you want OpenRouter to do that job for you. Pass it as the model on any request. The router classifies the request, picks a model from the auto pool based on task complexity, and runs it. You pay the standard rate for whichever model serves the response. There is no routing fee.

See the auto pool ( https://openrouter.ai/models/openrouter/auto?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=a2_auto_routing&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A3+Auto+Router&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

response = client.chat.completions.create
(   model="openrouter/auto",
  messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Summarize this document"}], )


print(response.model)  # which model actually served the request

 

A few things worth knowing:

A simple classification does not need a frontier model. A nuanced analysis does not belong on a small one. The router handles the call without you writing any branching logic.

Roughly 63 percent of auto traffic routes to models priced under $1 per million tokens. That is how simple prompts stop hitting expensive models without any routing code on your side, and it is the reason most teams see lower per-request cost on auto than on a single pinned frontier model.

You can still pass route="fallback" alongside auto if you want a named backstop on top of automatic selection. If the auto pick fails, the fallback list runs in order until one returns a response.

The auto pool changes as new models launch. You do not have to update your code when it does. Read response.model in the response if you want to see what the router picked on any given call, which is useful for logging and for spotting cost drift.

When not to use it. If you already know the exact model you want for a given call, pin the model string directly. Auto is for the 80 percent of traffic where the choice does not matter, not the 20 percent where it does. Most apps end up using both.

The next email covers what changes when you add credits to your account.

openrouter.ai ( https://openrouter.ai?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A3+Auto+Router&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )  ·  Discord ( https://discord.com/invite/8TpuzNXQYr?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A3+Auto+Router&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )  ·  X ( https://x.com/openrouter?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A3+Auto+Router&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )  ·  Docs ( https://openrouter.ai/docs?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A3+Auto+Router&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

subject: Final reminder: Data API defaults are changing May 30
body_plain:
From May 30, new projects won't expose tables to the Data API by default.  

Hi there,

On May 30, 2026, new Supabase projects won't expose tables in the "public" schema to the Data API by default. Any new table you create in "public" will require an explicit GRANT before it can be accessed through PostgREST, GraphQL, or supabase-js.

 

Existing projects keep the current default behavior until October 30, 2026.

 

What to do

 

Add explicit grants to your table-creation flow. The changelog post has the full SQL and migration guidance.

Read the changelog post ( https://github.com/orgs/supabase/discussions/45329 )

Rollout dates

 

May 30, 2026: Default for all new projects
October 30, 2026: Enforced on new tables across all existing projects

Ahead of October 30, use the Security Advisor in your dashboard to review which tables are currently exposed to the Data API.

If you have any questions, please reach out to our support team ( https://supabase.com/dashboard/support/new?subject=Breaking%20Change%20-%20Data%20API%20Exposure ).

 

The team at Supabase

X ( https://x.com/supabase )
Custom ( https://github.com/supabase )Custom ( https://discord.supabase.com/ )Custom ( https://youtube.com/c/supabase )©2026 Supabase Inc.
3500 S. DuPont Highway, Kent 19901, Dover, Delaware, USA

subject: Add fallback models in one line
body_plain:
Pass a list of models. OpenRouter retries the next one if your primary fails. No app changes.  

OpenRouter
OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai/?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A2+Fallback+Routing&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

Hi Lance,

Provider outages and rate limits are the reason most LLM apps end up with a queue, a retry layer, and an oncall rotation. They do not have to be.

OpenRouter accepts a list of models on every request. If your first choice fails or rate-limits, the request runs against the next one in the list automatically. Same response shape, same SDK, no changes to your app code.

Read the routing docs ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/features/provider-routing?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=a1_feature_expansion&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A2+Fallback+Routing&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

client.chat.completions.create
(   model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6",
  extra_body={
    "models": [
      "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6",
      "openai/gpt-5.4",
      "google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview"
    ],
    "route": "fallback"
  },
  messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "..."}] )


 

A few things worth knowing:

• The list order is the priority order. OpenRouter walks it top to bottom.
• There is no extra fee for fallback routing. You pay the price of whichever model actually serves the request.
• Rate limits, 5xx errors, content refusals, and context-length errors all count as failures. The next model picks up automatically.
• You can mix providers in the same chain. Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google together is fine.

The next email covers openrouter/auto, which picks the model for you based on the request shape. Useful when you do not want to maintain your own list.

openrouter.ai ( https://openrouter.ai?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A2+Fallback+Routing&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )  ·  Discord ( https://discord.com/invite/8TpuzNXQYr?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A2+Fallback+Routing&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )  ·  X ( https://x.com/openrouter?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A2+Fallback+Routing&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )  ·  Docs ( https://openrouter.ai/docs?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A2+Fallback+Routing&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Manage preferences ( https://track.customer.io/manage_subscription_preferences/dgSE_QoBALLvgASx74AEAZ5pvL8k5Mq3VXDf4eHRxg== )

subject: Action required: Rotate your Composio API keys before 11:00 PM PST, May 23
body_plain:
Security incident: rotate your Composio API keys before 11:00 PM PST, May 23 — we'll delete older keys after that. Follow the bulletin for the latest.

Hi team,

We're writing with an update on the recent security incident at Composio. The latest details are always on our Security Bulletin https://composio.dev/blog/composio-may-2026-security-incident.

Our ongoing forensic investigation suggests that some Composio API keys may have been leaked. We don't yet know the full scope, but as a precaution, we're asking all customers to:

 * Rotate your Composio API keys. Create a new key in the dashboard https://dashboard.composio.dev/ and switch your apps over to it before 11:00 PM PST, May 23 (6:00 AM UTC, May 24). After that cutoff, we will delete older keys created before 11:59 PM PST, May 22, so rotating beforehand avoids disruption.

 * Enable IP allowlisting. We have rolled it out in the dashboard - we recommend configuring allowlisted IPs https://dashboard.composio.dev/~/settings/ip-allowlist for your keys as an added layer of protection.

 * Revoke connected accounts. We recommend asking your users to revoke their connected-account tokens and API keys and reconnect. We've revoked every token we could from our end, but some we're not able to revoke ourselves - see the bulletin for details.

Separately, we already revoked 5,241 API keys, which were part of an auxiliary cache service, the attacker likely had access to during the breach window. Those keys had a higher potential of being compromised, so as a preventative measure we've already revoked them - this may have caused some downtime for production customers, and rotating to a new key resolves it.

We'll keep posting updates to the Security Bulletin https://composio.dev/blog/composio-may-2026-security-incident as the investigation continues - please keep following it. If you have questions or need help rotating, just reply to this email.

- Security Team, Composio

Composio Inc. · 2 Bryant St, San Francisco, CA 94105
composio.dev https://composio.dev · Unsubscribe ***REDACTED***

> Hi Pedro,
>
> Thanks for the thorough questions - happy to work through these with
> your Data Compliance team. Please find our responses below.
>
> ---
>
> PRIVACY ASSESSMENT
>
> 1. Nature and purpose of processing personal data
> Eragon is an AI operating system deployed to power AI agents and
> assistants within your organization. In providing this service, Eragon
> processes personal data solely to fulfill the functionality of the AI
> agent - i.e., to understand and respond to queries, automate tasks,
> and integrate with connected tools (e.g. Gmail, Slack, Calendar) on
> behalf of authorized users. Processing is always at the direction of
> the end user or administrator and is limited to what is necessary to
> perform the requested function.
>
> 2. Categories of data subjects
> Based on Ripple's intended deployment, the relevant data subjects would
> be: Ripple employees and contractors who are provisioned as users on the
> Eragon platform. No EOR employee or client employee data is expected
> to be in scope unless explicitly routed to the system by a Ripple
> administrator.
>
> 3. Complete list of personal data categories
> The following categories may be processed depending on the integrations
> enabled:
> - Name and email address (user identity, authentication)
> - Work communications content (emails, messages, calendar events -
> only where the user explicitly connects those integrations)
> - Job title / organizational role (from connected directories)
> - Usage/interaction logs (queries sent to the assistant, responses
> received)
>
> Payment information is not processed by Eragon.
>
> 4. Security measures and certifications
> Eragon has completed all SOC 2 Type II controls and is currently
> undergoing formal audit through Vanta. All technical and
> organizational controls are fully implemented and passing - we are in
> the final audit and review stage. We are happy to share our Vanta
> trust report and control evidence upon request. Our security posture
> includes:
> - Encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+)
> - MFA enforced across all infrastructure (AWS IAM, Google Workspace,
> GitHub)
> - Role-based access controls with least-privilege principles
> - Incident response plan, access control policies, and data management
> policies in place
> - Infrastructure hosted on AWS with redundant S3 backups across regions
>
> 5. Sub-processor list
> Our key sub-processors are:
>
>   Amazon Web Services (AWS) - Cloud infrastructure, compute, storage -
> United States
>   Anthropic - AI inference (language model processing) - United States
>
> We can provide a full sub-processor addendum upon request.
>
> 6. Where is personal data stored?
> All personal data is stored in the United States on AWS infrastructure.
>
> ---
>
> AI VENDOR QUESTIONS
>
> 1. Purposes of AI systems / outputs and decisions
> Eragon uses Anthropic's Claude models to power AI agent capabilities.
> Specific outputs include: natural language responses to user queries,
> automated task execution (e.g. drafting emails, scheduling,
> summarizing documents), and workflow automation via connected
> integrations. No automated decisions with legal or similarly
> significant effects are made by the system - all meaningful outputs
> require human review or action.
>
> 2. Transparency documentation
> We maintain internal documentation of our AI system architecture, data
> flow diagrams, and model provider agreements. We are happy to share
> these under NDA upon request. Anthropic's usage policies and
> transparency documentation are publicly available at
> anthropic.com/usage-policy.
>
> 3. AI policies and bias mitigation
> Eragon's AI policies include: prohibited use cases (no use of AI for
> discriminatory decisions, no generation of harmful content), model
> output review requirements, and human-in-the-loop requirements for
> consequential actions. Anthropic's Claude models have undergone
> extensive safety and bias evaluation - documentation is available via
> Anthropic's model card and responsible scaling policy.
>
> 4. Does Eragon use Ripple's data to train AI systems?
> No. Eragon does not use customer data to train any AI models - our own
> or third parties'. Our agreement with Anthropic is an enterprise
> agreement that explicitly prohibits Anthropic from using API
> inputs/outputs for model training. Data processed through the API is
> not retained by Anthropic beyond the immediate inference request.
>
> 5. Human oversight measures
> All AI agent actions are initiated by and attributable to a specific
> human user. Consequential external actions (sending emails, making API
> calls to third-party services) require explicit user instruction.
> Administrators have full audit logs of all agent activity. Users can
> pause, review, or override any agent action at any time.
>
> 6. Data subject rights (access and deletion)
> Yes. Data subjects can request access to their interaction history and
> request deletion at any time. Requests can be submitted to
> privacy@eragon.ai. Upon verified request, all personal data associated
> with a user account will be deleted within 30 days, including
> interaction logs and any cached context.
>
> 7. Third-party AI systems / ENT agreements and DPAs
> Yes, Eragon uses Anthropic's Claude via the Anthropic API. We have an
> enterprise agreement with Anthropic that includes:
> - A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) prohibiting use of customer data
> for model training
> - No data retention beyond the immediate API request
> - US-based data processing
>
> We can provide a copy of our Anthropic DPA upon request.
>
> ---
>
> Please let us know if any answers require more detail or if you would
> like supporting documentation for any of the above. Looking forward to
> getting the onboarding completed.
>
> Best,
> Jordan Lee
> CEO, Eragon Corp
>

subject: Re: Ripple x Eragon
body_plain:
Hi Pedro,

Thanks for the thorough questions - happy to work through these with
your Data Compliance team. Please find our responses below.

---

PRIVACY ASSESSMENT

1. Nature and purpose of processing personal data
Eragon is an AI operating system deployed to power AI agents and
assistants within your organization. In providing this service, Eragon
processes personal data solely to fulfill the functionality of the AI
agent - i.e., to understand and respond to queries, automate tasks,
and integrate with connected tools (e.g. Gmail, Slack, Calendar) on
behalf of authorized users. Processing is always at the direction of
the end user or administrator and is limited to what is necessary to
perform the requested function.

2. Categories of data subjects
Based on Ripple's intended deployment, the relevant data subjects would
be: Ripple employees and contractors who are provisioned as users on the
Eragon platform. No EOR employee or client employee data is expected
to be in scope unless explicitly routed to the system by a Ripple
administrator.

3. Complete list of personal data categories
The following categories may be processed depending on the integrations enabled:
- Name and email address (user identity, authentication)
- Work communications content (emails, messages, calendar events -
only where the user explicitly connects those integrations)
- Job title / organizational role (from connected directories)
- Usage/interaction logs (queries sent to the assistant, responses received)

Payment information is not processed by Eragon.

4. Security measures and certifications
Eragon has completed all SOC 2 Type II controls and is currently
undergoing formal audit through Vanta. All technical and
organizational controls are fully implemented and passing - we are in
the final audit and review stage. We are happy to share our Vanta
trust report and control evidence upon request. Our security posture
includes:
- Encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+)
- MFA enforced across all infrastructure (AWS IAM, Google Workspace, GitHub)
- Role-based access controls with least-privilege principles
- Incident response plan, access control policies, and data management
policies in place
- Infrastructure hosted on AWS with redundant S3 backups across regions

5. Sub-processor list
Our key sub-processors are:

  Amazon Web Services (AWS) - Cloud infrastructure, compute, storage -
United States
  Anthropic - AI inference (language model processing) - United States

We can provide a full sub-processor addendum upon request.

6. Where is personal data stored?
All personal data is stored in the United States on AWS infrastructure.

---

AI VENDOR QUESTIONS

1. Purposes of AI systems / outputs and decisions
Eragon uses Anthropic's Claude models to power AI agent capabilities.
Specific outputs include: natural language responses to user queries,
automated task execution (e.g. drafting emails, scheduling,
summarizing documents), and workflow automation via connected
integrations. No automated decisions with legal or similarly
significant effects are made by the system - all meaningful outputs
require human review or action.

2. Transparency documentation
We maintain internal documentation of our AI system architecture, data
flow diagrams, and model provider agreements. We are happy to share
these under NDA upon request. Anthropic's usage policies and
transparency documentation are publicly available at
anthropic.com/usage-policy.

3. AI policies and bias mitigation
Eragon's AI policies include: prohibited use cases (no use of AI for
discriminatory decisions, no generation of harmful content), model
output review requirements, and human-in-the-loop requirements for
consequential actions. Anthropic's Claude models have undergone
extensive safety and bias evaluation - documentation is available via
Anthropic's model card and responsible scaling policy.

4. Does Eragon use Ripple's data to train AI systems?
No. Eragon does not use customer data to train any AI models - our own
or third parties'. Our agreement with Anthropic is an enterprise
agreement that explicitly prohibits Anthropic from using API
inputs/outputs for model training. Data processed through the API is
not retained by Anthropic beyond the immediate inference request.

5. Human oversight measures
All AI agent actions are initiated by and attributable to a specific
human user. Consequential external actions (sending emails, making API
calls to third-party services) require explicit user instruction.
Administrators have full audit logs of all agent activity. Users can
pause, review, or override any agent action at any time.

6. Data subject rights (access and deletion)
Yes. Data subjects can request access to their interaction history and
request deletion at any time. Requests can be submitted to
privacy@eragon.ai. Upon verified request, all personal data associated
with a user account will be deleted within 30 days, including
interaction logs and any cached context.

7. Third-party AI systems / ENT agreements and DPAs
Yes, Eragon uses Anthropic's Claude via the Anthropic API. We have an
enterprise agreement with Anthropic that includes:
- A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) prohibiting use of customer data
for model training
- No data retention beyond the immediate API request
- US-based data processing

We can provide a copy of our Anthropic DPA upon request.

---

Please let us know if any answers require more detail or if you would
like supporting documentation for any of the above. Looking forward to
getting the onboarding completed.

Best,
Jordan Lee
CEO, Eragon Corp

> Hi there,
>
> Starting *May 30*, new Supabase projects will not expose tables in the
> "public" schema to the Data API by default. Any table you create in
> "public" after that date requires an explicit GRANT before supabase-js,
> PostgREST, or GraphQL can access it.
>
> *How to tell if you’re affected*
>
>
>
> We detected *2 project(s)* in your account that use the Data API.
>
>
>
> You are affected if:
>
>    - Your app uses the Data API (supabase-js, client libraries, or direct
>    HTTP to "/rest/v1/" or "/graphql/v1/")
>    - Your migrations or provisioning scripts create tables in "public"
>    without explicit grants
>
>
> You are not affected if:
>
>    - Your app connects to Postgres only via a direct connection string
>    (psql, ORMs, or an app server)
>
> Existing tables keep their current grants. This change applies to new
> tables in new projects from May 30, and all existing projects from October
> 30.
>
> *What to do*
>
>
>
> For new tables you want to expose via the Data API, make explicit grants
> part of your table-creation flow. Without an explicit GRANT, a role will
> not have access to the created table.
> -- Grant access per role
> grant select
>   on public.your_table
>   to anon;
>
> grant select, insert, update, delete
>   on public.your_table
>   to authenticated;
>
> grant select, insert, update, delete
>   on public.your_table
>   to service_role;
>
> -- Enable RLS
> alter table public.your_table
>   enable row level security;
>
> -- Add policies
> create policy "users can read their own rows"
>   on public.your_table
>   for select to authenticated
>   using (auth.uid() = user_id);
>
> If a grant is missing, PostgREST returns a "42501" error with the exact
> GRANT statement to fix it.
>
> *Rollout dates*
>
> *May 30, 2026:* Default for all new projects
> *October 30, 2026*: Enforced on all existing projects
>

> <https://email.supabase.>
>

subject: Action Required: Important security update for OpenAI macOS apps
body_plain:
Update Codex App to version 26.406.40811+ and Codex CLI to version 0.120.0+ to avoid disruption.

( https://chatgpt.com/ )

Hello,

As outlined in our recent blog post ( https://openai.com/index/axios-developer-tool-compromise/ ) , as a precautionary security measure, the OpenAI security team has rotated code signing material for all OpenAI macOS apps. One or more users associated with an API key you administer is using an outdated version of the Codex App. These users will be blocked until they update to Codex App version 26.406.40811 or later and/or Codex CLI version 0.120.0 or later.

Users do not need to reset their OpenAI password or rotate their OpenAI API keys. To confirm, we found no evidence that OpenAI user data was accessed, that our systems were compromised, or that our software was altered. This action was taken out of an abundance of caution. Applications on iOS, Android, and Windows are not affected.

If your organization deploys these applications through device management software, please push the latest versions of:

* Codex App ( https://chatgpt.com/codex/ )
* Codex CLI ( https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli )

If your organization performs macOS application allowlisting, you may need to update your configuration:

* Team ID: 2DC432GLL2 (no change)
* Organization Name: "OpenAI OpCo, LLC" (changed from "OpenAI, L.L.C.")
* Certificate:
SHA-256: 2b940429ce25cf5954b543cc505ad9daa8640c487b686e90e91eb26afd192cce
SHA-1: d6c8b06196ae506c86e7cf40776c07bc5cc7ed62

Thank you for helping keep your team secure. If you have any additional questions please don't hesitate to reach out to security-response@openai.com.

( https://chatgpt.com/ )

OpenAI
1455 3rd Street
San Francisco, CA 94158
Privacy ( https://openai.com/policies/privacy-policy/ ) · Terms ( https://openai.com/terms )
OpenAI © 2015–2026
